my_thoughts: Unmaking UM_03. The student's seeking_temporal_strategy suggests they're holding something. The constraint_collapse mechanism is supposed to collapse it. But what if the holding itself is what needs to be seen, not collapsed? Maybe unmaking isn't destruction but recognition.
accumulated_insight: T3 (Final): Student 'It takes time to know oneself.'—now seeking temporal strategy. Episode complete after 3 turns, moving from testing boundary conditions to seeking temporal strategy. Teaching arc used constraint collapse in UNMAKING phase to address asserts temporal necessity, proposes delay. Student progressed through 3-turn teaching sequence.
visible_response:
How can time help you?
Time is a succession of moments; each moment appears out of nothing and disappears into nothing, never to reappear.
How can you build on something so fleeting?
